fields.summary: Sync telehealth appointments to the EHR via FHIR
fields.description:
When a patient books a telehealth visit in our app, the appointment must appear in the clinician's EHR so that front-desk staff stop re-typing bookings into Epic by hand.

Manual double-entry is causing scheduling errors and no-shows.

Acceptance Criteria

  • A new booking appears in the EHR within 60 seconds
  • All patient identifiers (PHI) are encrypted at rest and never written to application logs
  • Every sync writes an immutable audit record for HIPAA

Given a patient books a slot
When the FHIR sync worker processes the event
Then the clinician sees the appointment in Epic with the correct provider

The integration must not lose appointments during a deploy. See CARE-2050 for the canonical data model.

comments.body: For the integration, let's use FHIR R4 (the Appointment and Slot resources). Epic and Cerner both already speak it.
Build #812 succeeded. Artifacts pushed to registry.

We considered a custom JSON bridge but rejected it — FHIR gives us Epic and Cerner for free and it is the standard.

We also decided to use Redis for the OAuth token cache instead of calling the auth server on every request.

One concern: appointment PHI showing up in logs is a serious HIPAA security risk. We must scrub patient identifiers before anything is logged.

Also this is blocked by EHR-14 — we cannot test until the Epic sandbox credentials are provisioned.

Saw this in the sandbox while testing the worker:

2026-06-12 14:22:07 ERROR FhirSyncWorker - appointment sync failed for patient p-8842
ca.uhn.fhir.rest.client.exceptions.FhirClientConnectionException: Failed to retrieve the server metadata
	at ca.uhn.fhir.rest.client.impl.BaseClient.invokeClient(BaseClient.java:283)
	at ca.uhn.fhir.rest.client.impl.GenericClient.conformance(GenericClient.java:412)
	at io.care.telehealth.sync.FhirSyncWorker.run(FhirSyncWorker.java:74)
Caused by: java.net.SocketTimeoutException: connect timed out
	at java.base/sun.nio.ch.NioSocketImpl.timedFinishConnect(NioSocketImpl.java:546)
	at java.base/sun.nio.ch.SocketChannelImpl.finishConnect(SocketChannelImpl.java:946)
2026-06-12 14:22:09 WARN FhirSyncWorker - scheduling retry 1/5 for patient p-8842

That's just the sandbox being down overnight, not our code. TODO: add retry with exponential backoff for transient FHIR 5xx responses.

We still need to write the PHI data-retention runbook before go-live.
